title: CulinaryOS
tagline: The operating system for the modern restaurant.
description: CulinaryOS is a web-based SaaS restaurant OS built in the Toast model — one platform covering the full service cycle: tablet POS, real-time Kitchen Display System, inventory and pantry tracking with automated purchase orders, end-of-day reporting, Stripe-powered payments, a customer-facing online ordering storefront, and an owner/manager back-office. KitchenKit and CulinaryOps connect as MCP servers, giving CulinaryOS AI-agent access across recipe scaling, prep planning, labor, food cost, vendor management, and waste tracking.
story: Built after years of watching kitchen staff juggle five different apps during a dinner rush. Every station had its own screen, its own tool, its own failure point. CulinaryOS started as a question: what if the whole kitchen ran on one OS? The Ratio Blueprint Engine — which stores recipes as relationships, not fixed quantities — is what makes it not just another POS.
journey: CulinaryOS is the project that everything else in the culinary ecosystem feeds into. It started as a simple question — why does a kitchen need five apps open during service? — and grew into a full platform architecture. The Ratio Blueprint Engine was the technical unlock: once recipes became ratio relationships instead of fixed quantities, the entire prep, scaling, and AI-agent layer became possible. KitchenKit and CulinaryOps were built alongside it as standalone tools that also snap in as MCP servers, so CulinaryOS becomes the hub without forcing operators into a closed ecosystem.
forWho: Restaurant owners, head chefs, and kitchen managers who need operations software built by someone who has actually worked the line.

detailedSpecs.architecture: pnpm monorepo managed by Turborepo. Five React 18 + Vite apps (pos, kds, admin, web, server) share a Hono API gateway, a Supabase PostgreSQL backend, and a suite of typed packages. Domain-split MCP servers expose the full operation layer to any MCP-compatible AI agent. KitchenKit and CulinaryOps plug in as external MCP servers, making CulinaryOS the unified hub across the entire culinary ecosystem.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Order placed in POS → event emitted to event-bus → KDS receives ticket via Supabase Realtime → ratio-engine scales prep quantities → completion event written to domain_events audit ledger → EOD reports aggregated from domain_events.
keyPatterns: Ratio Blueprint Engine — recipes stored as ratio relationships, not fixed quantities
Domain-split MCP servers — full operation layer exposed to any AI agent
KitchenKit + CulinaryOps as external MCP satellites
Supabase Realtime for live KDS ticket flow
Stripe integration for payments and online ordering
